background: Studies in the general population suggest that the gestational length of uncomplicated pregnancies varies according to a Normal distribution with μ=39 weeks with σ=2 weeks. A sample of 22 middle-class African American women demonstrates an average gestation length (x̄) of 38.5 weeks.

task.query: Test whether the mean gestation period in the African American women is significantly different from the expected value of 39 weeks. Use a two-sided alternative hypothesis. Show all hypothesis testing steps.
task.answer: The four-step procedure is used to solve the problem: A. Hypotheses. H0: μ=39 versus Ha: μ≠39 B. Test statistic. SE_x̄=2/√22=0.4264 and z_stat=(38.5-39)/0.4264=-1.17 C. P-value. One-sided P=Pr(Z≥1.17)=0.1210 [from Table B] and two-sided P=2×0.1210=0.2420. The evidence against H0 is nonsignificant by usual conventions. D. Significance level (optional). The results are not significant at α=0.10 (retain H0).

meta info.caption: Gestational length，African American women, hypothesis test.
